summary: Launch-eve regression for Track Order + admin status notes-prompt + email deep-link. Backend pytest 8/8 passing. Frontend admin overlay verified end-to-end (Path A 'Update & send' and Path B 'Skip — just update'). Public Track auto-track from URL params + amber 'NOTE FROM OUR TEAM' banner verified. Order reset to 'processing' after testing.

issues: React console warning: 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop' originating from AdminDashboard render — pre-existing, not from this feature

action_items: (Optional) Tidy the React 'unique key' warning in AdminDashboard render — pre-existing, unrelated to this launch feature.
critical_code_review_comments: OnlineOrders.jsx Skip path calls handleStatusUpdate('') — works because falsy notes are filtered out before PUT, but worth a comment for clarity.

Generic input selectors on /shop/track are visually safe but could be brittle for tests because the global header search input is rendered first in DOM — adding data-testid='track-order-input' / 'track-email-input' / 'track-submit-btn' would make e2e flows rock-solid.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (Did-You-Mean UI not directly automated due to header-search input selector collision; backend /api/shop/track/suggest endpoint verified returning the real order)

seed_data_creation: None. Used existing test order TS-260425-C455E9 (qasim3638@gmail.com).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest /app/backend/tests/test_track_order_notes.py covers: public track GET (case-insensitive + wrong-email rejected), /api/shop/track/suggest, PUT /api/shop/orders/{id}/status with notes (persistence + email_sent), PUT skip-notes, email.py deep-link string presence. Frontend admin overlay flow verified via [data-testid='view-order-...'] → status <select> → Update btn → [data-testid='status-notes-overlay'] → 'status-notes-input' / 'status-skip-notes-btn' / 'status-update-with-notes-btn'. Public auto-track verified via /shop/track?order=...&email=... — amber banner [data-testid='track-latest-note']. To test Did-You-Mean UI, fill the form inputs by placeholder rather than nth-of-type because the global header has its own search <input>. Order ended in 'processing'.
